item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved the dialog. I could literally hear Candler in my head as I read his words.

I'd also be interested to know what happened with New Coke. I'm thinking the Ethics Committee made them try to use dead animals instead of humans, and it turned out that this just wasn't good enough. Sadly, my skills as an author aren't up to the challenge, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Few things have ever come close to making me want to quit drinking Coke, but this… Was certainly a close contender to reaching that outcome. Good job on incorporating Norse mythology and American capitalism into an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this article Akimoto. From shorter, stranger stories, you've started dipping into more complex branching stories with numerous implications, and you're getting really good at balancing them all out.

It's been a thrill watching you develop as a writer since you joined the site, and can't wait to see more. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot. My only criticism would be around the first interview log, which felt a little modern to me. Maybe it could benefit from the dialogue being slightly more formal/in keeping with the period (although the pop factory line was a nice touch).

As others have said, the whole concept of the Foundation essentially partnering with a massive, murderous corporation for their mutual benefit is really great and very well handled.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In contrast to the previous featured skip from this series I actually like this one a lot. I’d like it even had it stopped at the “horrors of capitalism” part so cleverly showcased in the interview. But it actually goes a step further, to implicate the Foundation in a way that I find unsettlingly realistic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oddly enough this would explain those cravings I get for classic coke or coke zero (which is classic coke with non-caloric sweeteners), but not other colas or even the reformulated coke versions like diet coke (which is the 1980's new coke formula with non-caloric sweeteners).

It may be the synthetic 4677-2 speaking, but I give it a strong +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
November is the month of Dead god. Love this new canon so far, and can I just say I never thought I’d be creeped out by Coca Cola…

Enthusiastic +1

item.title: Bingo
item.content:encoded:
You, in fact, did interpret this correctly. In my head though, it's more or less they're studying the effect on a few Sites, like 10 or so. To see if the additive has the desired impact, hence the "Additive-Personnel Effect Study".

Also good catch on the taunt - taut thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The thing was strapped up like a cow for the slaughter, its tentacles tied and pulled taunt— a shit ton of hoses hooked to it." I think that should be 'taut.'

I really enjoyed reading this, and I love SCPs that really explore the internal function of the Foundation. I especially love that addendum right at the end - although I'm not sure the Foundation would take it so far as anomalously affecting all their employees. (If I even read that correctly - I'm not sure I did.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely amazing. The way this was done painted the perfect picture in my head and had me hooked instantly. +1

item.title: Really worked up an appetite
item.content:encoded:
This was genius, bizarre, and intriguing all at the same time. I love the message it was sending about corporate greed, and companies exploiting everything in their power to maximize profit. Candler's dialogue and expression were excellent, and I feel like you really nailed it with this one.

I'm in the mood for a can of coke now, thanks.

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Staff Review
item.content:encoded:
Well, I procrastinated on reading this in chat, so I'll make it up to you on site.

+ Crit
- Crit

Level 5/4677 Clearance

This may just be a headcanon difference, but I've always considered clearance levels to be:

  • 0 = all Foundation employees
  • 1 = Junior Researcher
  • 2 = Researcher
  • 3 = Lead/Senior Researcher
  • 4 = Site Director/Department Head
  • 5 = O5 Command/Ethics Committee

By this, it's strange to see a level 5 clearance individual asking for a level 4's permission, but like I said, it could all be chalked up to headcanon. Just thought I'd mention it.

Foundation contacts within historic academia will reinforce the popular belief that the original formula contained trace amounts of cocaine until it was changed in 1929.

This is an excellent hook. You have my interest.

the pseudo-occult fraternity named "The Order of Auðumbla"

I'd cut out "named" here, personally.

Anyway, the first set of rooms were i: machines, boxes, and a bunch of barrels, the type of things you'd expect at a pop factory.

Not sure what he's trying to say exactly here. Possible typo?

its tentacles tied and pulled taunt

taunt -> taut

Candler: [Exaggerated sigh.] I had no time to wait for the will of this monster. Simple supply and demand— and its supply was not meeting our demand. I did a test of my own to see if the… "departed"… would satisfy this creature's hunger. It did, in fact, did produce the same results— but the creature was not pleased. It fought back. Of course, some silly monster was no match for cold hard American steel— hence its bondage.

This line here really embodies what I liked so much about your characterization of Candler. This was spot on for Roaring 20's capitalism and it really drives home a central theme of exploitation.

Considering the vast financial benefit of an agreement with the Coca-Cola Company, using anomalous means the Foundation developed a synthetic compound that replicates the desired effects of SCP-4677-2.

I'd flip this sentence around a bit to read "Considering the vast financial benefit of an agreement with the Coca-Cola Company, the Foundation has developed a synthetic compound that replicates the desired effects of SCP-4677-2 using anomalous means." Just reads better.

Consumption of this compound leads to an increase in favoritism and the overall enjoyment of Coca-Cola causing a subtle psychological dependency on the beverage.

Missing comma after Coca-Cola.

The Foundation purposed to mass-produce this substance as required and despite not receiving SCP-4677 Mr. Candler agreed to these terms. In return, on 1928/6/29, the Foundation received a 20% holding in the Coca-Cola Company and ownership of SCP-4677 and SCP-4677-1, which contains the only copy of the rituals relating to SCP-4677.

I'd also restructure this bit to read "The Foundation has proposed to mass-produce this substance in return for 20% ownership in the Coca-Cola Company and the continued containment of SCP-4677 and SCP-4677-1." I think it's much more concise and easier to read.

On Sunday and Thursday, the Maintenance Team is to inspect, calibrate and activate the SCP-4677 restraint system.

I think "Sundays and Thursdays" would read better. Alternatively, consider giving an approximate time of day to maintain consistency with the rest of the document?

Overall, this was good and I enjoyed it very much. I dug the thematic elements, the gentle inclusion of your own GoI in the Church of Iðunn, the backdoor politics and slimy business dealings, seeing a weaker than usual Foundation caught on their back foot, and so on. There was a lot to like here and not a lot to distract me from said enjoyment outside of the odd headcanon conflict or syntaxial awkwardness. The final reveal that the Foundation is now containing and abusing -1 in much the same way that Coca-Cola was doing before, thus drawing the parallel between the two organizations, was expected, but honestly it served to the benefit of the piece. It's a little glimpse into the bleak reality of the situation. Never once was the deity even thought about as anything other than a cog in the greater machine. Ultimately, Candler was right—the Foundation was in no position to judge. Easy +1.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to TheMightyMcB or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, this is such a great knife's-edge balance of funny, bizarre, horrific, and surreal. Great stuff. I plan to make moo-ing noises now each time I crack open a Coke, to the deep consternation of all around me. +1

item.title: COKE, COW, AND CAPITALISM
item.content:encoded:
My Norse saga continues through this glimpse into the Foundation's past. A god caught between two opposing forces, a powerless prisoner of man's greed or a tool for the Foundation righteous cause. Highlighting a time when the capitalist juggernauts of America held unyielding political and economic power, while the Foundation was vulnerable to the influence and vaccinations of the outside world. I hope y'all enjoy.

Critique Credit placeHolder - until i remember all the people who crit this.

byebye
